Paper Title/Study: Effect of vitamin A adjunct therapy for cerebral malaria in children admitted to Mulago hospital: a randomized controlled trial
Authors: Mwanga-Amumpaire J, Ndeezi G, Tumwine JK
Medical Treatment: Intravenous quinine dihydrochloride, oral quinine sulphate.
Biomarkers: serum retinol, parasite density, hemoglobin, WBC count, platelets
Study Population: 142 children aged 6-59 months admitted with cerebral malaria.
Outcome / Finding: Coma recovery time, time for convulsions to stop, parasite clearance, fever clearance, mortality, time to start oral feeds.
Contribution: Problem: To determine the effect of vitamin A supplementation on treatment outcome of cerebral malaria in children.
Contribution: Result: Vitamin A did not significantly reduce coma duration, but there was a trend toward earlier resolution of convulsions and lower mortality (8.1% vs 16.2%) compared to placebo.

Paper Title/Study: A randomised, double blind, placebo controlled clinical trial of Vitamin A in severe malaria in hospitalised Mozambican children
Authors: Varandas L, Julien M, Gomes A, Rodrigues P, Van Lerberghe W, Malveiro F
Contribution: Problem: Effect of vitamin A in severe malaria in hospitalized children.
Contribution: Result: Reduction in risk of death noted; vitamin A did not influence coma recovery time.

Paper Title/Study: Effect of Vitamin A supplementation on morbidity due to plasmodium falciparum in young children in Papua New Guinea: a randomised trial
Outcome / Finding: Malaria episodes, positive blood slides, fever episodes
Contribution: Problem: Effect of vitamin A supplementation on morbidity due to P. falciparum.
Contribution: Result: Significant reduction in malaria episodes and lower positive blood slides in the vitamin A group.
